source_metadata.title_raw: Bianca Pirayou - A-19-048 - May 5, 2019 - San Jose

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 5, 2019

Bianca Pirayou
Pirayou Law
6469 Almaden Expressway #80-125
San Jose, CA 95120

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-19-048

Dear Ms. Pirayou:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Please note that we only advise on provisions of the Act. Therefore, we offer no opinion on
the application, if any, of Elections Code Section 18680 or any other law outside of the Act to your
questions.

Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for
additional advice.

QUESTIONS

1. Must Dr. Hernandez's Candidate Controlled Ballot Measure Committee ("Committee")
continue to be identified as a candidate controlled ballot measure committee now that Dr.
Hernandez is no longer in office?

2. May Dr. Hernandez expend Committee funds for certain healthcare related purposes such as
communications addressing public healthcare needs, future initiatives, or contributing to a
nonprofit for scholarships for healthcare students?

3. Is the Committee subject to the rules in Regulation 18521.5(d)(3) regarding permissible use
of remaining campaign funds 60 days prior to termination?

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-19-048
Page No. 2

CONCLUSIONS

1. Dr. Hernandez's Candidate Controlled Ballot Measure committee must remain a candidate controlled ballot measure committee until Dr. Hernandez no longer has an open committee for his own election to office.

2. Because the Committee is candidate controlled it's use of funds is restricted as explained below.

3. Regulation 18521.5(d)(3) restricts the Committee's expenditures prior to termination and is applicable to the Committee 60 days prior to when it plans to change its status from a candidate controlled ballot measure committee to a non-candidate controlled ballot measure committee.

FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER

You serve as legal counsel to former Senator Dr. Ed Hernandez and his candidate-controlled ballot measure committee, Senator Ed Hernandez Ballot Measure committee – Yes on Measure LP ("Committee").

Dr. Hernandez previously served as a Senator representing the 22nd Senate District. His last day in elective office as Senator was November 30, 2018. He ran unsuccessfully for Lieutenant Governor in the November 2018 General Election and his candidate controlled committee for that election remains active, though he has plans to terminate that committee soon. The Committee changed its status in September 2018 from a primarily-formed ballot measure committee in Los Angeles County to a primarily formed ballot measure committee in support of Measure LP in the City of La Puente. The Committee's current cash balance is approximately $70,000. He wishes to change the status of the Committee to a state general purpose ballot measure committee in support of healthcare issues and related measurers and change its name to California Healthcare Initiative.

Dr. Hernandez proposes to expend Committee funds for the following purposes:

• Social media and other forms of communication to inform the public on healthcare issues facing the state of California.

• Possible future initiatives to help all Californian's attain access to healthcare.

• A contribution to a non-profit organization established by Dr. Hernandez that grants scholarships to Latino students who are interested or will be pursuing a career in the health fields.

You would like to know whether Dr. Hernandez is required to include his name or wording indicating that the Committee is a ballot measure committee in the Committee's new name and whether the Committee must continue to be identified as a candidate-controlled committee. Also,

File No. A-19-048
Page No. 3

you inquire as to whether the Committee is subject to Regulation 18521.5(d)(3) regarding
permissible use of remaining funds 60 days prior to termination.

ANALYSIS

Section 82007 defines the term "candidate" which includes:

"An individual who is listed on the ballot or who has qualified to have write-in
votes on his or her behalf counted by election officials, for nomination for or
election to any elective office …"

Section 82007 also provides that "an individual who becomes a candidate shall
retain his or her status as a candidate until such time as that status is terminated pursuant
to Section 84214."

Dr. Hernandez is an individual that was listed on the ballot for nomination to elective office
and Dr. Hernandez has not yet terminated his candidate controlled committee for election to the
office of Lieutenant Governor, though he lost the November 2018 election. Therefore, Dr.
Hernandez remains a "candidate" under the Act. Until his committee for election to office is
terminated, Dr. Hernandez remains a candidate and the Committee must retain its status as a
candidate controlled ballot measure committee.² When Dr. Hernandez's candidate controlled
committee for his election to office is terminated, the Committee may terminate its status as a
candidate controlled committee. At that time, the Committee would be required to file an amended
Statement of Organization (Form 410) to reflect that change.³

You have also inquired about the use of campaign funds by the Committee and whether they
may be used for the following purposes:

• Social media and other forms of communication to inform the public on healthcare issues
facing the state of California.

• Possible future initiative to help all Californian's attain access to healthcare.

• A contribution to a non-profit organization established by Dr. Hernandez that grants
scholarships to Latino students who are interested or will be pursuing a career in the health
field.

Regulation 18521.5(d)(1) & (2) provide the permissible uses of campaign funds held by
candidate controlled ballot measure committees prior to the committee preparing to terminate. The
regulation provides that the committee may use its campaign funds to return a contribution to a

² Section 84214 and Regulation 18404 provide the process by which a committee may be terminated.

³While changing the Committee from a candidate controlled ballot measure committee to a general purpose
committee will terminate the ballot measure committee for all effective purposes, we find it permissible to simply
amend the Committee's Form 410 to reflect the change in committee status and unnecessary to file a separate
termination statement.

File No. A-19-048
Page No. 4

contributor. Otherwise, unless a committee is preparing to terminate its status as a committee, the
funds "must" be used:

".... only to make expenditures related to a state or local measure or potential
measure anticipated by the committee, or to qualification or pre-qualification
activities relating to such measures. Such expenditures include, but are not limited
to, payment of the committee's reasonable and ordinary operating expenses,
administrative overhead, fundraising activities, travel, compliance costs, and
attorney's fees incurred as a result of the committee's activities."

Therefore, so long as the Committee remains a candidate controlled ballot measure
committee, Committee funds may be used to inform the public on healthcare issues facing the state
of California, or a possible future initiative to help all Californian's attain access to healthcare, to
the extent the expenditure relates to a State or local measure anticipated by the committee.
However, under Regulation 18521.5, the funds may not be used to make a contribution to a non-
profit organization until the committee is preparing to terminate.

Regulation 18521.5(d)(3) provides for the permissible uses of campaign funds held by a
candidate controlled ballot measure committee that is preparing to terminate its status as a
committee:

"(3) A committee that is preparing to terminate its status as a committee may, at
any time within 60 days prior to the effective date of its termination, disburse
some or all of its leftover funds in either of the following ways:

(A) Pursuant to subdivision (b)(3) of Section 89519.

(B) To a political party committee, so long as the funds are not used for a
contribution as defined in Section 82015 to a candidate, or for a communication
which expressly advocates the nomination, election or defeat of a clearly
identified candidate as defined in Section 82031."

Section 89519(b)(3) provides that campaign funds may be used in the following way:

"(3) Donations to any bona fide charitable, educational, civic, religious, or similar
tax-exempt, nonprofit organization, where no substantial part of the proceeds will
have a material financial effect on the former candidate or elected officer, any
member of his or her immediate family, or his or her campaign treasurer."

When Dr. Hernandez terminates his candidate controlled committee for election to office,
the Committee may change its status to a noncandidate controlled ballot measure committee.
Because the committee would be in effect terminating its status as a candidate controlled ballot
measure committee, Regulation 18521.5(d)(3) would be applicable and the Committee may use its
funds as provided in that portion of the regulation. We caution that when making a contribution to a
bona fide charitable nonprofit organization there must be no material financial effect on Dr.
Hernandez or any member of his immediate family or his campaign treasurer.

File No. A-19-048
Page No. 5

While the Commission has not defined the term "material financial effect" in the context of
uses of campaign funds, it has done so in the context of the Act's regulation of conflicts of interest.
Generally, these regulations provide that a transaction has a material financial effect if the official
or the official's immediate family members will receive a measurable financial benefit or loss from
the decision. (Regulation 18702.5.) (Forcina Advice Letter, No. A-16-232.) To the extent that Dr.
Hernandez or an immediate family member of Dr. Hernandez receives income from the nonprofit, it
is possible that there may be a material financial effect on him or his immediate family.
Accordingly, if Dr. Hernandez or an immediate family member of Dr. Hernandez receive or will
receive income from the nonprofit in the future, he should seek additional advice prior to
contributing to the nonprofit pursuant to Regulation 18521.5(d)(3).

We note that once the Committee changes its status to a non-candidate controlled ballot
measure committee, Regulation 18521.5 (d)(1)-(3) would not apply to the use of the Committee's
campaign funds that are raised after the change in status. Section 89512.5 would apply and requires
that expenditures by a non-candidate controlled committee shall be reasonably related to a political,
legislative or governmental purpose unless the expenditure confers a substantial benefit on any
individual or individuals with authority to approve the expenditure of campaign funds held by the
committee. In which case, the expenditure must be directly related to a political, legislative or
governmental purpose. If Dr. Hernandez has any questions regarding the permissible use of funds
raised after the Committee's change in status, he should seek additional advice.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Sukhi K. Brar
Supervising Counsel, Legal Division

SKB:aja

l. 
Dr. Hernandez's Candidate Controlled Ballot Measure committee must remain a candidate 
controlled ballot measure committee until Dr. Hernandez no longer has an open 
committee for his own election to office. 
2. 
Because the Committee is candidate controlled it's use of funds is restricted as explained 
below. 
3. 
Regulation 18521.5(d)(3) restricts the Committee's expenditures prior to termination and is 
applicable to the Committee 60 days prior to when it plans to change its status from a 
candidate controlled ballot measure committee to a non-candidate controlled ballot measure 
committee.

contributor. Otherwise, unless a committee is preparing to terminate its status as a committee, the 
funds "must" be used:
